title: eriocephalus africanus var. africanus
intro: Eriocephalus africanus var. africanus, commonly known as the African bush tea or wild rosemary, is a shrub native to the Western Cape province in South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eriocephalus africanus var. africanus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to leverage its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
